name: OctoCode — GitHub Code Analysis MCP
what_it_does: OctoCode MCP server enabling AI agents to perform semantic code search and analysis across GitHub repositories — searching code semantically, analyzing repository structure, finding function definitions and usages, and integrating deep GitHub code intelligence into agent-driven code review and development research workflows.
use_cases: Semantic code search across GitHub repositories from research agents
Finding function definitions and usage patterns from code analysis agents
Exploring unfamiliar codebases from onboarding agents
Cross-repository code pattern discovery from architecture analysis agents
Finding examples and implementations from coding assistant agents
Analyzing repository structure and dependencies from audit agents
not_for: Private repositories without GitHub access tokens
Real-time code execution (this is analysis/search only)
Teams preferring official GitHub Copilot or GitHub MCP server
best_when: An agent needs to search and analyze code across GitHub repositories with semantic understanding — beyond simple text search into structural code analysis.
avoid_when: You only need basic GitHub API access — the official GitHub MCP server is more comprehensive for general GitHub operations.

auth.notes: GitHub personal access token (PAT) required for private repositories and higher rate limits. Public repo search may work without auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: GitHub API: 60 requests/hour unauthenticated, 5,000/hour with PAT
pricing.notes: Community MCP is free. GitHub API has generous authenticated rate limits. GitHub account recommended.

known_agent_gotchas: GitHub API rate limits are per-token — shared PATs can be exhausted quickly
Semantic code search quality depends on indexing — not all repos are equally searchable
Private repos require PAT with repo scope — minimal scope recommended
Community MCP from mcp-ecosystem — quality higher than average
Large repos with complex histories can produce slow or incomplete results
Code search differs from text search — understand GitHub's code search syntax
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (GitHub API). Store GitHub PAT as environment variable. Minimum required PAT scopes. Community MCP. Do not expose PAT in logs. Private repo access requires careful scope management.

security_score_components.security_notes: GitHub API HTTPS. PAT with minimum scopes. Community MCP. Secure PAT storage.
